title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE RURAL HEALTH UNIT IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF STO. TOMAS, PROVINCE OF PAMPANGA, INTO A FIVE-BED CAPACITY HEALTH INFIRMARY, TO BE KNOWN AS THE STO. TOMAS HEALTH INFIRMARY, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 23220 / 88 OG No. 23, 3504 (June 8, 1992)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 7424, April 14, 1992 ]

AN ACT CONVERTING THE RURAL HEALTH UNIT IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF STO. TOMAS, PROVINCE OF PAMPANGA, INTO A FIVE-BED CAPACITY HEALTH INFIRMARY, TO BE KNOWN AS THE STO. TOMAS HEALTH INFIRMARY, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The rural health unit in the Municipality of Sto. Tomas, Province of Pampanga, is hereby converted into a five-bed capacity health infirmary, to be known as the Sto. Tomas Health Infirmary.

SEC. 2. The amount necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act shall be included in the General Appropriations Act of the year following its enactment into law and thereafter.

SEC. 3. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved,

(Sgd.) NEPTALI A. GONZALES

(Sgd.)

RAMON V. MITRA

President of the Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on October 2, 1991 and February 3, 1992, respectively.

(Sgd.)

ANACLETO D. BADOY, JR.

(Sgd.)

CAMILO L. SABIO

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: APR 14 1992

(Sgd.)

CORAZON C. AQUINO

President of the Philippines
